title: vatica mangachapoi var. parvifolia
intro: Vatica mangachapoi var. parvifolia, also known as the Sumatran mangrove tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian herbal medicine, particularly in the Philippines and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and inflammation, and its extracts have been used to treat various ailments, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Vatica mangachapoi var. parvifolia are typically made from its leaves, bark, and roots, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or decoctions, or applied topically as ointments or creams.
